note: Scores are updated with each new data package. Scale: 0% = no data / falsified, 100% = reproducible with controls, falsifiable, pre-registered.
changelog: v6→v10: DDM Monte-Carlo, Wong-Wang attractor, spatial routing falsification, flux-bridge confirmation. P1 +27, C4 +13, C5 +7, P3 +5, C1 +2. New claim H11.

claims.name: Two-Stage Architecture
claims.desc: Stage-I netting (GKSL transport) + Stage-II fixation (DDM or attractor commitment).
claims.evidence: DDM MC (N=741, 1000 trials/subj): d=-1.34, 90.8% faster, ΔRT=-12ms, Δacc=+2.7%. WW attractor (N=741, 500 trials/subj): d=-1.84, 96.6% faster, ΔRT=-111ms, Δacc=+6.7%. G_log dose-response r≈0.5. Topology disruption DDM: MS 84%, WS 100%. Flux-bridge confirmed 22/22. CRN bias = starting-point prior.
claims.gap: β/κ not biophysically calibrated. I₀=0.38 near-bifurcation. One dataset (HCP). r²≈0.27 (73% variance unexplained).
claims.next: ε-sweep × G_log correlation, biophysical κ calibration, second dataset (Drosophila MB closed-loop).

claims.name: GKSL as Stage-I proxy
claims.desc: Lindblad open-system formalism correctly interpolates between coherent and diffusive regimes on biological graphs.
claims.evidence: ~520K trials across 6 disorder levels. CTRW ≡ 0. Block2 hierarchy: telegraph 0/20, Schrödinger 72/100, GKSL 100/100.
claims.gap: Alternative wave proxies limited to telegraph only.

claims.name: Mechanistic Neutrality
claims.desc: CRN scalar R₀ interface is substrate-neutral — any transport mechanism generating R₀ feeds commitment.
claims.evidence: WW results: CRN bias = starting-point prior, not coherence-specific channel. Three-component model (transport × label × commitment) neutral to substrate. Block2: telegraph 0/20 → Schrödinger 72/100 → GKSL 100/100.
claims.gap: Only GKSL tested as full Stage-I source. No alternative transport mechanism producing nonzero R₀.

claims.name: Four-Factor Mechanism
claims.desc: Stage-I efficiency depends on interaction of partial coherence, graph topology, disorder, and commitment topology.
claims.evidence: WW dose-response r≈0.5 confirms graded dependence. DDM + WW = two commitment topologies, both work. 3 organisms + 741 HCP subjects.
claims.gap: No experimental confirmation. Fourth factor (commitment topology) tested with only two models.

claims.name: Disorder-Enhanced Selectivity (DES)
claims.desc: Moderate disorder ε improves target selectivity via inverted-U.
claims.evidence: Works on micro-connectomes. DES = 56% topology + 26% weight-specific (p=0.002). MB Perm v5b running.
claims.gap: DDM/WW tests at ε=0 only. DES ε-sweep not tested in closed-loop.

claims.name: ENAQT Window (inverted-U in κ)
claims.desc: Optimal dephasing rate κ* maximizes transport — neither fully coherent nor fully classical.
claims.evidence: 7-point κ grid + ICC fix + CTRW null. 741/1064 invU (70%). G median=0.78. Rock-solid.
claims.gap: Single DTI modality. No experimental dephasing manipulation.

claims.name: Topology Dependence
claims.desc: ENAQT gain depends on native connectome topology — disruption destroys advantage.
claims.evidence: Gate1c (5 subj × 12 seeds): MS 99.5% (428/430), WS 94.4% (406/430). DDM topology disruption: MS 84%, WS 100%. Two independent tests (GKSL + DDM).
claims.gap: Predictive model ΔG~f(graph) absent. MB permutation pending. N>5 needed for DDM topology disruption.

claims.name: Energy Advantage
claims.desc: CRN architecture saves energy through faster commitment at equal or better accuracy.
claims.evidence: DDM accuracy-matched: ΔRT=-12ms, 100% faster at equal P. WW: 96.6% faster + 96.9% more accurate. Fewer errors = fewer repeat trials.
claims.gap: No explicit energy accounting (joules). No comparison with alternative architectures.

claims.name: ENAQT on human DTI connectome
claims.desc: Environment-assisted transport operates on macro-scale DTI connectomes.
claims.evidence: 1064 HCP subjects, ~520K GKSL trials. 741 invU (70%). ICC = 0.914.

claims.name: Containment (T3)
claims.desc: Near falsified.
claims.evidence: T3 containment not supported.

claims.name: DES on T2
claims.desc: DES confirmed on gating (T2) circuits.
claims.evidence: DES = 56% topology + 26% weight-specific.

claims.name: No DES on T3
claims.desc: No DES on relay (T3) circuits.
claims.evidence: Confirmed on T3 circuits.

claims.name: Gain vs CRW (full)
claims.desc: Full gain comparison vs continuous random walk.
claims.evidence: Limited.

claims.name: κ-grid adequacy
claims.desc: 7-point κ grid sufficient to capture inverted-U.
claims.evidence: Settled.

claims.name: Pathway specificity
claims.desc: ENAQT advantage is pathway-specific within connectomes.
claims.evidence: Confirmed across basal ganglia and motor pathways.

claims.name: DES topology-driven
claims.desc: DES depends on topology, not just disorder level.
claims.evidence: MB Perm v5b pending.

claims.name: T3 emerging ws
claims.desc: T3 effect emerging with weight-specific analysis.
claims.evidence: Preliminary.

claims.name: Bilateral symmetry
claims.desc: ENAQT selectivity shows bilateral symmetry across hemispheres.
claims.evidence: T3R fix pending.

claims.name: Mouse ENAQT-negative
claims.desc: Mouse cortex connectome does not show inverted-U (ENAQT-negative).
claims.evidence: 0/37 invU. Predictor: λ₂>1 AND 0.2<CV<0.5.

claims.name: Label field necessity
claims.desc: Label field is necessary for target-specific transport.
claims.evidence: Confirmed in model.

claims.name: Closed-loop decision advantage
claims.desc: Stage-I ENAQT transport, via scalar R₀ interface, accelerates Stage-II commitment in two independent formalisms (DDM, Wong-Wang) at cohort scale (N=741).
claims.evidence: DDM MC d=-1.34, 90.8% wins. WW d=-1.84, 96.6% wins. Topology disruption destroys advantage. G_log dose-response r≈0.5.
claims.gap: β/κ not calibrated. One dataset. Starting-point prior, not drift. No in vivo behavioral correlate.
